key_insight: Achiral, D4h-symmetric zinc phthalocyanine can spontaneously break symmetry in supramolecular condensates, producing emergent helicity and chiroptical activity.
engineering_relevance: This points to a route for designing self-assembled chiral materials from nominally achiral molecules for applications in chiroptics, spin-selective devices, and quantum materials.
method: M06/DGDZVP ab initio DFT to probe intermolecularly induced structural relaxation.
Confocal Raman Optical Activity (ROA) to detect chiroptical signatures.
Mode analysis of aza-bridge and pyrrole-ring vibrations to track symmetry breaking.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a red shift in the aza-bridge and pyrrole stretching modes from DFT, and a negative Cotton effect at 1505 cm^-1 and 1338 cm^-1 in ROA measurements, supporting emergent supramolecular chirality.
watch_item: The result is demonstrated for one molecular system and relies on subtle spectral shifts, so broader generalization and robustness across environments still need validation.
paper_title: Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking and Emergent Helicity in Achiral D4h-Symmetric Zinc Phthalocyanine Condensates
